title: Interactive Example Truss Stiffness Solver
text:
The stiffness method is regularly used in the analysis of both statically determinate and indeterminate structures. While the method follows a clear set of steps, the process can feel long, confusing, and difficult at first. It also requires the use of linear algebra, which may feel daunting upon seeing it for the first time. The purpose of this interactive documentation is to help you gain confidence with using the stiffness method by allowing you to explore each of the steps of the method both on a member-by-member basis and for the structure as a whole.
Select a predefined truss for evaluation. Then, input material and section properties and prescribe loads on the nodes. Afterwards, click on a particular member to see how its length, orientation, and Young’s modulus affect its local stiffness matrices and ultimately its contribution to the global system of equations. You can also see how various loads and prescribed displacements influence resulting unknown reactions and displacements. Finally, after computing global unknowns, you can see their influence on your selected member’s forces at the bottom of the document.






Tip



Adjust Units per pixel and Units label to convert your computer’s pixel lengths into your preferred units (e.g., meters).

title: Interactive Example Truss Stiffness Solver
text:

\(\lambda_x\): — \(\lambda_y\): —


  Nodal loads

    
    
    Show node labels
    

  
  

  
    Arrow scale:  px per unit
    Force units: 
    Displacement units: 
    Clear all
  


Element Stiffness Matrix: Local Coordinate System
\[\begin{equation}
\mathbf{k}'_e = \dfrac{EA}{L}
\begin{bmatrix}
1 & -1 \\
-1 & 1
\end{bmatrix}
\end{equation}\]


Displacement Transformation Matrix
\[\begin{equation}
\mathbf{T}_e =
\begin{bmatrix}
\lambda_x & \lambda_y & 0 & 0 \\
0 & 0 & \lambda_x & \lambda_y
\end{bmatrix}
\end{equation}\]


Force Transformation Matrix
\[\begin{equation}
\mathbf{T}^T_e =
\begin{bmatrix}
\lambda_x & 0 \\
\lambda_y & 0 \\
0 & \lambda_x \\
0 & \lambda_y
\end{bmatrix}
\end{equation}\]


Element Stiffness Matrix: Global Coordinate System
\[\begin{align}
\mathbf{k}_e &= \mathbf{T}^T \mathbf{k}'_e \mathbf{T}\\
    & =\dfrac{EA}{L} \begin{bmatrix}
     \lambda_x^2 & \lambda_x \lambda_y & -\lambda_x^2 & -\lambda_x \lambda_y\\
     \lambda_x \lambda_y & \lambda_y^2 & -\lambda_x \lambda_y & -\lambda_y^2\\
     -\lambda_x^2 & -\lambda_x \lambda_y & \lambda_x^2 & \lambda_x \lambda_y\\
     -\lambda_x \lambda_y & -\lambda_y^2 & \lambda_x \lambda_y & \lambda_y^2
    \end{bmatrix}
\end{align}\]
